overview: This Pauper quadrant deck leverages Faithless Looting to rapidly dig for its core pieces: Inside Out, Tireless Tribe, and Sneaky Snacker. By reversing the text boxes of the tribe and the snacker, the deck creates a recursive loop that generates infinite +1/+1 counters, trample, and card draw. The goal is to assemble the combo quickly and execute it before the opponent can stabilize or interact.
win_conditions: Executing the Inside Out reversal combo on Tireless Tribe and Sneaky Snacker to generate infinite value and lethal damage
Overwhelming the opponent with a massively pumped, trampling Tireless Tribe
Drawing into lethal card advantage or combo pieces via Faithless Looting and Sneaky Snacker triggers

keep_criteria: Hands containing Faithless Looting or multiple low-cost cantrips
Hands with at least two combo pieces (Inside Out, Tireless Tribe, Sneaky Snacker)
Hands with early disruption or cheap creatures to protect the combo
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking Faithless Looting or any combo piece
Hands with only high-cost cards or no lands
Hands with multiple removal spells but no way to deploy them early

early_game: Play lands and cheap creatures to establish board presence
Cast Faithless Looting on turn 2 or 3 to dig for combo pieces
Avoid unnecessary combat that could lose key pieces to removal
mid_game: Assemble the Inside Out, Tireless Tribe, and Sneaky Snacker trio
Use card draw and counters to outvalue the opponent
Protect combo pieces with cheap interaction or evasion
late_game: Execute the reversal combo to generate infinite counters and draw
Attack with the pumped Tireless Tribe to close out the game
Use excess card advantage to find answers to late-game threats

key_cards.name: Faithless Looting
key_cards.role: Primary combo enabler and dig tool
key_cards.notes: Cast on turn 2 or 3 to discard two cards and draw four, rapidly finding Inside Out, Tireless Tribe, and Sneaky Snacker. Manage graveyard carefully to avoid opponent interaction.

key_cards.name: Tireless Tribe
key_cards.role: Core combo piece and win condition
key_cards.notes: Attack with it to trigger its ability. When reversed by Inside Out, the reordered text enables a recursive loop with Sneaky Snacker to generate infinite counters and trample.

key_cards.name: Sneaky Snacker
key_cards.role: Secondary combo piece and card draw engine
key_cards.notes: Works in tandem with the reversed Tireless Tribe to create the infinite loop. Its reversed text allows continuous counter placement and card draw without leaving the battlefield.

sequencing_tips: Always cast Faithless Looting before deploying your combo pieces to maximize dig potential
Hold up mana for potential counterspells or removal before committing to the combo
Reverse Tireless Tribe first, then Sneaky Snacker, to ensure the trigger order resolves correctly
Attack with the reversed Tribe immediately after assembling the loop to avoid opponent interaction

matchups.advice: Prioritize early disruption and use Faithless Looting to find cheap blockers or the combo quickly. Avoid trading key pieces unnecessarily.
Use card draw to outlast their resources. Protect combo pieces with evasion or counterspells. Execute the combo before they can assemble a full board of answers.
Race them by going wide with cheaper creatures while digging for the combo. Use Sneaky Snacker for incremental value to outdraw their curve.

common_threats: Board wipes that remove Tireless Tribe and Sneaky Snacker
Counterspells that prevent Inside Out or Faithless Looting from resolving
Hand disruption that delays combo assembly
Direct damage spells that bypass the combo's defensive value

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Archetype: Inside Out Combo, Format: pauper, Colors: W/U/B/R, Signature cards: Tireless Tribe, Faithless Looting, Sneaky Snacker

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Signature cards and archetype name imply a recursive value loop and combat-focused finish

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Standard combo sequencing principles applied to the provided signature cards and Inside Out mechanics

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: General Pauper format meta knowledge applied to combo deck vulnerabilities
